summary: PR #1364 completed the JObj decompilation match by deleting the remaining sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s assembly file, switching jobj.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, and replacing the obj_files.mk entry so the build uses src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.c.o. The only C-side logic change shown is a cleanup in HSD_JObjUnrefThis: a local alternate iref_DEC helper was removed after object.h's ref_DEC and iref_DEC inline forms were reshaped to produce the needed codegen.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s, removing 6373 lines of handwritten/remaining assembly for JObj.
Changed configure.py so sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.c is declared Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
Changed obj_files.mk so the text object list uses $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s.o.
Removed local static inline helpers iref_none and iref_DEC_alt from src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.c.
Changed HSD_JObjUnrefThis to call the shared iref_DEC(jobj) helper rather than the JObj-local iref_DEC_alt(jobj).
Adjusted object.h inline refcount helpers ref_DEC and iref_DEC from a direct initialized bool cast form to an explicit bool variable assigned inside the if condition.
smart_moves: Instead of keeping a JObj-specific alternate inline helper just to satisfy codegen, the PR fixed the shared object.h inline helper shape and then reused iref_DEC directly in HSD_JObjUnrefThis.
The build was updated in both matching metadata paths: configure.py marks the object as Matching and obj_files.mk switches from the asm object to the C object.
The small header-level change removed duplicated logic in jobj.c while preserving the matching-sensitive evaluation pattern for ref_count and ref_count_individual checks.
decomp_lessons: For matching-sensitive inline helpers, small expression-shape differences matter. In this PR, changing `bool ret = ...; if (ret)` to `bool ret; if ((ret = ...))` was enough to eliminate a local alternate helper and let callers use the common iref_DEC.
When a source file becomes fully matching, remove the corresponding asm file and update both the object status configuration and the object-file list so future builds compile the C version.
Prefer fixing common inline utilities when the mismatch is caused by reusable helper codegen; this avoids accumulating one-off `_alt` helpers in individual decomp files.
A fully matched module PR can be mostly build/cleanup churn: the large visible diff may be assembly deletion, with only a few C/header lines explaining the final matching tactic.
naming_conventions: Existing baselib object refcount helpers use short names such as ref_DEC, ref_CNT, iref_DEC, and iref_CNT.
The removed temporary helper was named iref_DEC_alt and documented as an alternate form to match HSD_JObjUnrefThis; this PR moved away from that `_alt` convention once the shared helper matched.
JObj public symbols in the diff use the HSD_JObj prefix, for example HSD_JObjUnrefThis, HSD_JObjUnref, HSD_JObjCheckDepend, HSD_JObjResetRST, and HSD_JObjWalkTree.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the remaining JObj assembly after the C source matched.
Used inline-helper expression restructuring in object.h to influence generated code without changing the helper's apparent semantics.
Replaced a file-local alternate decrement helper with the shared iref_DEC after matching its codegen-sensitive form.

The deleted assembly covered many JObj routines and data, including symbols visible in the diff excerpt such as HSD_JObjCheckDepend, JObjResetRST, HSD_JObjResetRST, HSD_JObjWalkTree0, and HSD_JObjWalkTree.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided slice, so no human review rationale or requested changes can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other files with local `_alt` inline helpers that can be eliminated by reshaping shared helpers.
Search for remaining Object(NonMatching) sysdolphin/baselib files still backed by asm entries.
Audit object.h refcount helper call sites for matching-sensitive codegen after the ref_DEC and iref_DEC expression-shape change.
